title: Scott Bessent: Fixing the Fed, Tariffs for National Security, Solving Affordability in 2026
description:
(0:00) Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent joins the show

(0:55) Recapping 2025 and the state of the economy

(3:13) Tariffs: Leverage, legal challenges, implementation

(15:20) Affordability: inflation, BLS data, interest rates

(23:00) The Fed: biggest mistakes, how we got a 15 year asset bubble, rate cycle, appetite for US debt, Fed Chair candidates

(42:44) Focus on Main Street, taking equity stakes in American companies

(50:40) Tax cuts, Trump accounts, economic legacy

analysis.summary: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent joins the show to discuss the state of the economy, tariffs, affordability, and the Fed. They cover topics such as inflation, interest rates, and the biggest mistakes made by the Fed. The conversation also touches on taking equity stakes in American companies and tax cuts.

takeaways.text: Tariffs can be used as leverage
Inflation is a major concern for affordability
The Fed's biggest mistakes led to a 15-year asset bubble
Taking equity stakes in American companies can help Main Street
Tax cuts can have a significant impact on the economy
The Fed Chair candidates will play a crucial role in shaping the economy
Interest rates are a key factor in affordability
The economy is facing significant challenges in 2026

sentiment.notes: The conversation is cautious due to the significant challenges facing the economy in 2026, but there are also discussions about potential solutions and a sense of optimism about the possibility of positive change.

notable_moments.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent joins the show
Discussion about the Fed's biggest mistakes and the 15-year asset bubble
